steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says readers who aren’t familiar with Spanish can easily read both Cisneros’s and Castillo’s works. For that conclusion to follow, Castillo’s approach must be the same as Cisneros’s — implying Spanish meaning through surrounding English text.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — unfamiliar to: If Castillo’s approach were unfamiliar to Cisneros’s, the two works would handle Spanish differently, and readers couldn’t necessarily read both easily. ✗
  • Choice B — frequent in: Frequency of Spanish phrases in Cisneros’s work is never established as the key feature, so saying Castillo’s approach is “frequent in” Cisneros’s doesn’t logically set up the conclusion. ✗
  • Choice C — difficult for: If Castillo’s approach were difficult for Cisneros’s approach, neither interpretation makes sense grammatically or logically. ✗
  • Choice D — similar to: If both authors use the same strategy — letting surrounding English imply the meaning of Spanish words — then non-Spanish readers can access both works equally. This directly supports the conclusion. ✓
That is the correct answer.
